key_drivers: A soft multi-session USD trend is still supporting metals, equity index futures, and non-USD FX even though the Fed signal remains a neutral hold.
VIX remains in a balanced regime but is falling on the day, while SOX strength and a lower MOVE index are selectively favoring growth-sensitive risk assets.
Crude inventories posted a draw, but ceasefire and Iran-deal headlines are easing the geopolitical oil premium and capping upside in energy.

stale_sources: CME FedWatch was not directly accessible during this run, so the Fed stance used a same-week verified neutral-hold read.
Atlanta Fed GDPNow was last updated on 2026-04-09 and remains the latest official estimate.
FRED DFII10 and BAMLH0A0HYM2 latest observations are dated 2026-04-15, reflecting normal reporting lag.
World Gold Council ETF-flow evidence is still the March 2026 monthly release.
fallbacks_used: BOE Report Reuters pickup was used to confirm the April 15 EIA crude inventory draw while the official EIA landing page only exposed report links in extracted text.
TradingEconomics was used for the copper factor instead of Investing.com.
TradingEconomics was used to confirm the latest China manufacturing PMI slowdown for the AUD-specific growth factor.
